question: What is XR (Extended Reality)? Extended Reality (XR) is an umbrella term that includes all immersive technologies that blend or replace the physical world with digital content. It covers Virtual Reality (VR), Augmented Reality (AR), and Mixed Reality (MR), each offering different levels of immersion and interaction. In the context of this tool, XR defines the overall direction of the project, influencing every decision that follows, from how content is created to how it is experienced and delivered to users. Choose which XR Type you would like to work with

answers.title: VR Virtual Reality
answers.description: Virtual reality (VR) places users inside a fully digital environment using a headset. It relies on real-time 3D systems and often requires higher processing power for immersive interaction.

answers.title: AR Augmented Reality
answers.description: Augmented reality (AR) overlays digital content onto the real world through devices like smartphones or tablets. It favours mobile-friendly tools and lightweight assets, making it an accessible and cost-effective way to enhance real-world experiences.

answers.title: MR Mixed Reality
answers.description: Mixed reality (MR) blends digital and physical environments, allowing virtual objects to interact with the real world in real time. It requires advanced hardware and spatial mapping but provides highly immersive experiences.

question: Choose from the following ways to create an object to go into your environment, this will be included in your XR project workflow.

answers.title: 3D Object Scan
answers.description: 3D scanning captures real-world objects or environments and converts them into digital 3D models using cameras or specialized sensors. It speeds up asset creation for XR experiences but often requires optimization afterward to ensure performance.

answers.title: 3D Object Modelling
answers.description: 3D modeling creates digital objects from scratch using specialized software. It allows full creative control for custom experiences but often takes more time and skill than using existing assets.

answers.title: 3D Object Asset Library
answers.description: Asset libraries provide ready-made 3D models, textures, and animations for use in projects. They speed up development and cut costs, making them ideal for rapid prototyping or beginners.

question: Choose from the following ways to create an environment for your XR project workflow.

answers.title: 3D Environment Scan
answers.description: Using 3D scanning for environments captures real-world spaces and turns them into immersive digital scenes. It enables realistic recreations but may require optimisation for performance.

answers.title: 3D Environment Model
answers.description: Creating an environment through 3D modelling allows designers to build fully customised virtual worlds. It offers maximum creative control but usually requires more time and effort.

answers.title: Environment Asset Library
answers.description: Using an asset library for environments builds scenes from pre-made components like buildings or landscapes. It’s fast and cost-effective but can limit uniqueness.

question: Choose the way you would like your user to experience your XR project from the following platforms.

answers.title: Mobile
answers.description: A mobile app platform delivers XR experiences through smartphones or tablets. It offers wide accessibility with lower hardware requirements and broad audience reach.

answers.title: Web browser
answers.description: A web browser platform runs XR experiences directly without downloads. It enables quick sharing and easy access for a wide audience.

answers.title: Headset
answers.description: A headset platform delivers immersive XR experiences through dedicated VR or MR devices. It offers high immersion and interaction but requires advanced development and hardware considerations.

answers.title: Games console
answers.description: A games console platform delivers XR experiences through consoles, often with VR hardware. It enables high-performance, polished experiences but comes with stricter development requirements.
